title: AFT survival target for laps-until-next-pit, then convert to next-lap probability
summary: This label is naturally a time-to-event problem: many rows are one lap before a stop, many are earlier in the stint, and final stint rows are effectively right-censored if no later stop occurs.
rationale: This label is naturally a time-to-event problem: many rows are one lap before a stop, many are earlier in the stint, and final stint rows are effectively right-censored if no later stop occurs. XGBoost’s AFT objective is built for censored event-time prediction, and recent F1 pit-stop work reports useful gains from jointly modeling pit timing rather than only a binary next-lap label.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race sequence, derive `laps_until_next_pit`; for rows with no later stop in that race, use right-censoring at remaining laps. Train `xgboost.train(objective='survival:aft')` on the same tabular features plus stint-state features, then transform predicted survival time into `P(T<=1)` or rank-normalize it and blend with the existing binary model.
expected_effect: Should capture pit-window timing structure better than rowwise binary loss and improve ranking around the true stop lap, especially for late-stint examples.
risk: Needs careful label construction and probability mapping; AFT optimizes survival likelihood, not AUC directly, so it may need blending rather than standalone use.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
